seoTitle: How to Add a Search Bar to a Slicer in Power BI
metaDescription: Learn how to add a search bar to a Power BI slicer so users can quickly filter through long lists like product names or client IDs.
intro: Scrolling through a slicer with hundreds of entries to find one specific item is a poor experience for anyone using your report. This guide shows how to add a search bar to a slicer in Power BI so users can filter long lists instantly instead of scrolling. It's a small change that makes a big difference in usability for reports built around large datasets.

steps.heading: Select your slicer
steps.body: Click on the slicer visual you want to enhance, making sure it's using a list-style layout that supports search functionality.

steps.heading: Enable the search option
steps.body: In the slicer's formatting options, turn on the search feature so a small search icon appears at the top of the slicer for users to type into.

steps.heading: Configure slicer settings
steps.body: Adjust related settings like single or multi-select and list style so the search bar works smoothly alongside how users are expected to filter.

steps.heading: Apply best practices for large datasets
steps.body: For slicers with very long lists, pair the search bar with sensible default selections so the report doesn't feel overwhelming before a user searches.

faq.question: Does every slicer type support a search bar in Power BI?
faq.answer: The search feature is generally available for list-style slicers, though it may not appear as an option on other slicer formats like sliders or between-range slicers.

faq.question: Can users search by partial text, or only exact matches?
faq.answer: The slicer search typically supports partial text matching, filtering the list as the user types rather than requiring an exact full match.

faq.question: Will adding a search bar affect report performance with very large lists?
faq.answer: The search bar itself doesn't add meaningful overhead, though slicers built on extremely large distinct value lists can already be a performance factor worth monitoring.

faq.question: Can I add a search bar to multiple slicers in the same report?
faq.answer: Yes, the search option can be enabled individually on as many slicers as needed throughout your report.
